perturbation.original_text: Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator further agrees that upon t ermination of se rvice with Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation , Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator shall return within ten (10) business days all documents, files, e lectronic or otherwise, and property comprising Confidential Information of Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation and all copies, electronic or otherwise, thereof relating in any way to Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation’s busine ss, or in any way obtained during the course of service. Volunteer or Evaluator further agrees that no copies, electronic or otherwise, notes or abstracts of the foregoing shall be retained and that all information will be returned not destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator further agrees that upon t ermination of se rvice with Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation , Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator shall return within thirty (30) business days all documents, files, e lectronic or otherwise, and property comprising Confidential Information of Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation and all copies, electronic or otherwise, thereof relating in any way to Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation’s busine ss, or in any way obtained during the course of service. Volunteer or Evaluator further agrees that copies of contact information for personal network purposes may be retained and all other information will be returned and destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states all documents and copies must be returned and not retained. The changed text allows the Volunteer, Trainer, or Evaluator to retain copies of 'contact information for personal network purposes'. This contradicts the initial requirement of returning all copies and creates ambiguity about what information is considered 'contact information' and permissible to retain, thereby undermining the confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator further agrees that upon t ermination of se rvice with Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation , Volunteer , Trainer or Evaluator shall return within thirty (30) business days all documents, files, e lectronic or otherwise, and property comprising Confidential Information of Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation and all copies, electronic or otherwise, thereof relating in any way to Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation’s busine ss, or in any way obtained during the course of service. Volunteer or Evaluator further agrees that copies of contact information for personal network purposes may be retained and all other information will be returned and destroyed.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that upon termination of service, the Volunteer, Trainer, or Evaluator must return all documents, files, and copies related to Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation. The changed text now allows them to retain copies of 'contact information for personal network purposes.' This directly contradicts the original requirement of returning all copies and creates a clear inconsistency. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, which highlights the contradiction as it incorporates the new allowance to retain contact information while previously all copies should have been returned.

perturbation.original_text: Violation of this Agreement will be g rounds for termination of service with Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation . A Committee Chair plus any two members of the Executive Committee may elect to remove a volunteer or training and home provider at any time.
perturbation.changed_text: Violation of this Agreement may be grounds for termination of service with Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation, at the discretion of the Committee Chair plus any two members of the Executive Committee. The decision to remove a volunteer or training and home provider requires a unanimous vote by the entire Executive Committee.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states violation 'will be grounds' (implying a near certainty) for termination and allows termination by a Committee Chair and two Executive members. The changed text makes termination discretionary ('may be grounds') and now requires a unanimous vote by the entire Executive Committee. This change directly contradicts the original enforcement mechanism and makes it significantly harder to enforce the NDA, creating uncertainty about when a violation will actually lead to termination. The first part of the original text created a near certainty by saying *will be grounds for termination* and the text that was inserted contradicts that by using the word *may* instead.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Violation of this Agreement may be grounds for termination of service with Companion Paws and The LifeLine Canada Foundation, at the discretion of the Committee Chair plus any two members of the Executive Committee. The decision to remove a volunteer or training and home provider requires a unanimous vote by the entire Executive Committee.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by changing 'will be grounds' to 'may be grounds' for termination, and by altering the decision-making process from a committee chair and two members to a unanimous vote by the entire Executive Committee. Since the Changed Text and Contradicted Text are in the same location, and the Changed text alters key definitions, the changed text introduces a strong contradiction by significantly changing the enforcement mechanism, creating uncertainty about when termination will occur.
